title: cassia javanica subsp. renigera
intro: Cassia javanica subsp. renigera, also known as Senna auriculata, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. Cassia javanica subsp. renigera is believed to offer various health benefits, including the treatment of digestive disorders, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. It can be used in herbal preparations such as tea, capsule, or powder, often in combination with other herbs, to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits.
